purpose: Parse CSV text into structured rows with headers, column metadata, and deterministic parse errors for imports and data processing.

endpoints.description: Parse CSV text into JSON rows using caller supplied delimiter and header options.

dependencies.purpose: Parse CSV according to common quoting, delimiter, and newline rules

sidecar_specification.purpose: CSV-to-JSON parser for import and processing workflows.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when converting uploaded or generated CSV text into structured JSON rows.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for XLSX files, streaming huge datasets, or formulas that need spreadsheet recalculation.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Receive csv_text from an upload or fixture. 2) Call /csv/parse. 3) Validate rows with validation.json_schema_validator.

failure_modes.recovery: Fix CSV quoting or delimiter settings

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use csv-parse/sync parse with columns set from has_header. Default delimiter to comma. Trim UTF-8 BOM before parsing and return deterministic 400 errors for thrown parser exceptions.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: CSV fields can contain commas, quotes, and newlines. Splitting on comma manually will corrupt valid CSV.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Use a real CSV parser and table driven tests for quoted fields, CRLF newlines, empty values, and BOM input.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not parse CSV with string.split(',') and do not evaluate formula-looking cell values.
